frontier_justification: Generative molecular design for drug-like compounds is an active research area in 2025-2026. Language model approaches (MolGPT, ChemLLM) and 3D diffusion methods (GeoDiff, TargetDiff) compete to generate synthesizable, target-binding molecules.

papers.title: Automatic Chemical Design Using a Data-Driven Continuous Representation of Molecules
papers.key_contribution: First continuous latent space for molecules (SMILES VAE); enables gradient-based molecular optimization in latent space; Bayesian optimization over molecular properties.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Molecular space is discrete (graphs/SMILES); cannot apply gradient-based optimization for drug property optimization directly.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: VAE encodes SMILES strings to continuous latent space z; decoder generates SMILES from z; Gaussian process surrogate models property in latent space; Bayesian optimization.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Finds molecules with optimized logP and QED scores in latent space; continuous optimization produces valid molecules; demonstrates drug-like property optimization.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: SMILES VAE generates many invalid molecules; latent space not semantically organized; ignores 3D structure; no binding target conditioning.

papers.title: Junction Tree Variational Autoencoder for Molecular Graph Generation
papers.key_contribution: JT-VAE: decomposes molecular graph into junction tree of ring/chain substructures; generates valid molecules by tree-then-graph assembly; 100% validity guaranteed.

idea_genome.problem_genome: SMILES VAE generates many invalid SMILES strings; no guarantee of chemical validity during generation.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Two-level VAE: encode/decode junction tree (scaffold) then assemble full graph via subgraph attachment; validity enforced by construction.
idea_genome.observation_genome: 100% validity; better optimization of molecular properties than SMILES VAE; enables constrained optimization (modify scaffold while preserving ring system).
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Substructure vocabulary must be predefined; limited to 2D graph topology; no 3D conformation; no explicit binding target conditioning.

papers.title: Graph Convolutional Policy Network for Goal-Directed Molecular Graph Generation
papers.key_contribution: GCPN: reinforcement learning over stepwise molecular graph construction; reward combines molecular property score with chemical validity and drug-likeness constraints.

idea_genome.problem_genome: JT-VAE is VAE-based and requires expensive posterior optimization; cannot directly optimize non-differentiable molecular property objectives.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: RL agent adds atoms/bonds sequentially to build molecular graph; GCN encodes current graph state; reward from property scores + validity penalties; PPO training.
idea_genome.observation_genome: GCPN generates molecules with drug-like properties (QED, penalized logP) superior to JT-VAE; directly optimizes property targets.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: RL is sample-inefficient; still 2D graph only; no protein target conditioning; exploration in discrete molecular space remains challenging.

papers.title: TargetDiff: 3D Equivariant Diffusion for Target-Aware Molecule Generation and Affinity Prediction
papers.key_contribution: TargetDiff: generates 3D molecules conditioned on protein binding pocket; equivariant diffusion over atom types and 3D positions; explicit pocket-aware molecule design.

idea_genome.problem_genome: GCPN generates 2D molecules without 3D awareness; cannot design molecules that fit into specific protein binding pockets.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: SE(3)-equivariant diffusion over atom positions and types; conditions generation on protein pocket point cloud; joint denoising of molecule geometry and chemistry.
idea_genome.observation_genome: TargetDiff generates binding-aware 3D molecules for target proteins; significantly outperforms 2D methods in protein-ligand binding affinity prediction.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Slow generation (many diffusion steps); synthesizability not guaranteed; limited to the training distribution of pocket geometries.

papers.title: Navigating the Design Space of Equivariant Diffusion-Based Generative Models for De Novo 3D Molecule Generation
papers.key_contribution: Systematic study of design choices in equivariant diffusion for 3D molecule generation; introduces EDM++ with improved noise schedules and architecture; synthesizability conditioning.

idea_genome.problem_genome: TargetDiff and EDM have many design choices (architecture, noise schedule, conditioning); no systematic analysis of what matters most for generation quality.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Ablation study of: noise schedules (linear, cosine, learned), equivariant architectures (E-GNN, EGNN, PaiNN), conditioning strategies; incorporates synthesizability score.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Cosine noise schedule + PaiNN architecture + synthesizability reward significantly improves drug-like generation; provides design guidelines for 3D mol-gen.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Still requires known protein pocket; challenging optimization of multiple objectives (binding, synthesizability, toxicity) simultaneously.

papers.title: Breaking the Bottlenecks: Scalable Diffusion Models for 3D Molecular Generation
papers.key_contribution: Directly Denoising Diffusion Model (DDDM): replaces stochastic MCMC reverse steps with deterministic denoising via Reverse Transition Kernel (RTK); substantially reduces inference time for 3D equivariant diffusion; achieves faster convergence and superior structural fidelity on GEOM-DRUGS without sacrificing chemical validity.

idea_genome.problem_genome: EDM++ and TargetDiff use stochastic MCMC reverse sampling requiring many denoising steps; numerical instability and stochastic variance slow convergence and limit scalability.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Reverse Transition Kernel (RTK) framework reframes DDDM as deterministic denoising; well-conditioned kernels ensure numerical stability; SE(3)-equivariant denoisers maintain geometric validity; removes stochastic variance from inference.
idea_genome.observation_genome: RTK-guided deterministic denoising achieves faster convergence and superior structural fidelity compared to stochastic alternatives; maintains chemical validity on GEOM-DRUGS; code and models publicly available.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Reverse Transition Kernel requires pre-computed forward process statistics; conditioning on target protein pocket at inference time requires forward process recomputation, limiting real-time conditional generation.

edges.evolution_focus: JT-VAE mutates SMILES VAE by replacing string encoding with junction-tree molecular decomposition; same VAE latent optimization paradigm, enforces validity by construction.
GCPN hybridizes graph generation (from JT-VAE) with reinforcement learning (from RL literature); direct reward optimization replaces VAE posterior sampling.
TargetDiff replaces 2D graph RL generation with 3D equivariant diffusion conditioned on protein pocket; paradigm shift from topology generation to 3D structure-aware molecular design.
EDM++ systematically improves TargetDiff's diffusion framework via better noise schedules and architecture choices; adds synthesizability conditioning; same equivariant diffusion paradigm.

DDDM improves EDM++'s inference efficiency via deterministic Reverse Transition Kernel denoising; same SE(3)-equivariant diffusion paradigm, replaces stochastic MCMC with numerically stable deterministic denoising.

open_question: DDDM accelerates 3D molecular generation via deterministic denoising but the Reverse Transition Kernel requires pre-computed forward process statistics. What mechanism would enable real-time conditional molecular generation for previously unseen targets while jointly preserving 3D validity, pocket interaction constraints, ADMET feasibility, and synthesizability without forward-process recomputation?

question.name: Question
question.description: Present only the domain/subfield and the curated May-2026 research question; ask the model to propose a next-step research direction in Computational Chemistry / Generative Molecular Design.

library.name: Library
library.description: Present the curated research frontier plus 6 trace papers in random order with paper titles and brief abstracts, but without explicit lineage order, genome fields, or dynamics labels; ask the model to propose the next research direction.

lineage.name: Lineage
lineage.description: Present the curated research frontier plus the ordered trace with genome metadata, GenomeDiff-style change evidence, and parent-child relationships; ask the model to predict the next evolutionary step without naming predefined category labels.

frontier_evidence.title: Breaking the Bottlenecks: Scalable Diffusion Models for 3D Molecular Generation
frontier_evidence.unresolved_gap: Reverse Transition Kernel requires pre-computed forward process statistics; conditioning on target protein pocket at inference time requires forward process recomputation, limiting real-time conditional generation.
frontier_evidence.why_frontier: Used as the May 2026 frontier anchor: the arena question targets a limitation that remains unresolved at the endpoint of this lineage.

frontier_status.manual_review: Re-audited on 2026-05-05 for appendix release: active arena trace has a 2025/2026 frontier endpoint, a non-solved open research question, valid forward lineage edges, and generation settings aligned with post-hoc dynamics evaluation.
frontier_status.prompt_role: The same research question is shown in Question, Library, and Lineage settings; only the supporting literature/lineage context changes.
